description:
In RateLimiter.retry_after, when times is not empty, it calculates the oldest timestamp and returns @window_seconds - elapsed. If elapsed > @window_seconds (shouldn't happen if cleanup is working), the result could be negative, but we use .max which clamps to 1.

However, there's a potential issue: if the oldest timestamp is very old (cleanup failed), we could get a very small retry_after, like 1 second. This could allow rapid retry attempts.

Also, if window_seconds is 0 (edge case), we return @window_seconds which is 0.

Location: src/rate_limiter.cr:retry_after
title: Bug: RateLimiter retry_after returns 0 for valid token
